methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Andromeda Surgical is a Seed-stage biotech company founded by Nick Damiano and Kartik Tiwari that has raised $10M+. The valuation analysis draws from a broader Biotech & Health sector universe comprising 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, and Freenome.

Given data constraints, only the Stage-Progression method is applicable. Using the $10M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields $25M. A Peer-Median approach is not viable; while 16 same-sector peers exist in the dataset, none satisfy the narrow filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier combinations), requiring a minimum of 3 datapoints. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be deployed because Andromeda's revenue is not on file—standard practice for private companies at this maturity.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $21M–$29M, centered at $25M. This range reflects typical magnitude bounds around the core Stage-Progression output. Key uncertainty stems from the reliance on a single methodology; a meaningful second validation would require either Andromeda's revenue disclosure (enabling public-comp P/S application) or identification of 3+ closely-matched private-company transaction comparables. The $10M+ raised figure also warrants clarification on whether this represents fully deployed capital or committed amounts.
